goal: Become a strong system designer in backend + AI infrastructure

picker_rules.principle: Nothing is ever locked. All topics are always pickable, and free-typed custom topics always work. The graph only shapes SCORING of the 3 presented options.

scoring.momentum: graph distance to the last 1-3 completed sessions (builds_on + related edges, both directions). Did LLM-as-judge yesterday -> eval-infra-at-scale scores high today.
scoring.coverage_gap: how far the topic's track is below its mix_target given completed history
scoring.weak_area_overlap: topic concepts overlap with non-retired weak_areas
scoring.readiness: fraction of builds_on topics completed. LOW readiness reduces score but never blocks — if picked cold, the lesson backfills the missing fundamentals inline
scoring.review_synergy: small boost if the topic naturally exercises material due for spaced review
scoring.noise: rotation-seeded randomness so options stay fresh

picker_rules.option_1: momentum pick — highest graph-relevance to what you studied most recently
picker_rules.option_2: gap pick — highest coverage_gap + weak_area score, from a different track than option 1
picker_rules.override: typing any custom topic still bypasses everything, unchanged from today

tracks.title: Distributed Systems Core
tracks.why: The vocabulary and building blocks every HLD answer is assembled from. Zero coverage so far — this is the biggest gap.

ladder.title: Storage Engines: B-Trees vs LSM Trees, WAL, and Compaction
concepts: B-tree page splits and write amplification

ladder.title: Replication & Consistency: Leader/Follower, Quorums, Linearizability
concepts: sync vs async replication and failover data loss
read-your-writes and monotonic reads in practice

ladder.title: Raft: Leader Election, Log Replication, and Split-Brain Prevention
concepts: where Raft lives: etcd, Kafka KRaft, CockroachDB

ladder.title: Distributed Transactions: 2PC, Sagas, and the Outbox Pattern
Partitioning: Consistent Hashing, Virtual Nodes, and Rebalancing

capstone.title: Design Session: Distributed Job Scheduler (cron at scale)
capstone.prompt: Design a distributed job scheduler: 10M scheduled jobs, at-least-once execution, jobs must fire within 5s of schedule, workers crash routinely. You design first — components, data model, partitioning, failure modes, capacity math — then get critiqued against a rubric.
capstone.requires_note: soft gate: offer when most requires[] are done; never hard-block

tracks.ladder_note: order is a suggested progression only — scoring, not gating

tracks.title: Backend at Scale
tracks.why: The production-hardening layer: what keeps a service alive under load. One rung already done (circuit breakers).

ladder.title: PostgreSQL Internals: MVCC, VACUUM, and the Query Planner
concepts: planner estimates and when they lie

ladder.title: Backpressure: Connection Pools, Queue Depth, and Load Shedding
Caching at Scale: Invalidation, Stampedes, and Consistency
concepts: TTL jitter and probabilistic early refresh
cache consistency with the DB (versioning, CDC)

ladder.title: Kafka: Partitioning, Consumer Groups, and Exactly-Once Semantics
concepts: consumer lag as a capacity signal

ladder.title: Circuit Breakers, Bulkheads & Resilience: Half-Open State Machine
ladder.note: weakest score so far — bulkhead sizing flagged; capstone should lean on it

ladder.title: Distributed Rate Limiting: Token Bucket, Sliding Window, and Fairness
concepts: token bucket vs sliding window counter
Redis-backed distributed limiter and race conditions

ladder.title: Observability: SLOs, Error Budgets, and Distributed Tracing

capstone.title: Design Session: Multi-Tenant API Gateway
capstone.prompt: Design an API gateway for 200 tenants: per-tenant rate limits and quotas, p99 < 50ms overhead, graceful behavior when a backend degrades, full request tracing. Capacity math required: QPS -> connections -> pool sizes -> instances.
capstone.requires_note: soft gate: offer when most requires[] are done; never hard-block

tracks.ladder_note: order is a suggested progression only — scoring, not gating

tracks.title: AI Infrastructure Design
tracks.why: Your differentiator: backend/system-design skill applied to LLM systems. Three rungs already done (eval, agent memory, agent architectures); one in progress.

ladder.title: LLM Serving System Design: Router, Scheduler, and Autoscaling End-to-End
concepts: continuous batching scheduler as a queueing system

ladder.title: GPU Capacity Planning: Tokens/sec, Batching Economics, and Cost Math
concepts: prefill vs decode FLOPs and the roofline
ladder.note: directly reinforces weak areas: acceptance math, bulkhead sizing (same estimation muscle)

ladder.title: Semantic Caching Architecture: Embedding Keys, Thresholds, and Invalidation
concepts: similarity threshold tuning and false-hit cost
cache key design for chat vs completion
eviction and staleness for model upgrades
where it sits: gateway vs app layer

ladder.title: LLM-as-Judge: Building Reliable Eval Pipelines
Eval Infrastructure at Scale: Regression Gates, Sampling, and Feedback Loops
concepts: eval as CI: golden sets and regression gates

ladder.title: Agent Memory Architectures: Episodic, Semantic, and Procedural Stores
ReAct vs Plan-Execute Agents: Reflection Loops and Error Recovery
Multi-Agent Orchestration: Fan-Out, Shared State, and Failure Handling
Streaming Inference Pipelines: SSE/gRPC Streams, Backpressure, and Cancellation

capstone.title: Design Session: Multi-Tenant LLM Inference Platform
capstone.prompt: Design an inference platform: 50 tenants, mixed 7B/70B models, p99 < 800ms, bursty traffic, per-tenant quotas, semantic cache in front, eval feedback loop behind. You design: components, GPU capacity math, batching/routing strategy, failure modes, degradation policy. Critique rubric: requirements elicited, numbers add up, bottleneck identified, tradeoffs articulated.
capstone.requires_note: soft gate: offer when most requires[] are done; never hard-block

tracks.ladder_note: order is a suggested progression only — scoring, not gating

tracks.title: Wildcard Pool (LLM Architecture & Research)
tracks.why: Unordered exploration slot — keeps the serendipity of the current system. No ladder, no prerequisites.

ladder.title: Speculative Decoding: EAGLE-2 Draft Trees and Acceptance Math
ladder.note: no verified score (quiz UI was broken) — re-quiz via /grill

ladder.title: Mixture of Experts: Architecture, Sparse Routing, and Production Tradeoffs
ladder.note: no verified score (quiz UI was broken) — re-quiz via /grill

ladder.title: RoPE, YaRN, and NTK Scaling: Long-Context Extension
FlashAttention: Tiling, Online Softmax, and IO-Awareness
Quantization in Production: GPTQ, AWQ, FP8, and Accuracy Cliffs
Tensor Parallelism Internals: All-Reduce, Column/Row Sharding, Comm Overlap
ladder.note: was too hard cold — gated behind GPU capacity planning so the math lands

ladder.title: Mechanistic Interpretability: Induction Heads, Superposition, and SAEs

tracks.ladder_note: order is a suggested progression only — scoring, not gating

capacity_estimation.description: Back-of-envelope warm-up drills before each lesson: QPS -> connections -> memory -> shards; tokens/sec -> GPUs -> $/1M tokens. Directly trains the flagged weak areas (acceptance math, bulkhead sizing).
